orgName: ACT Mental Health Consumer Network (ACT MHCN)
name: Representation & Systemic Advocacy
description: Consumer-led representation and advocacy to government, advisory bodies, and service forums to influence mental health policy and system design.

name: Targeted Regional Initiatives for Suicide Prevention
description: A CHN commissioning program that funds and procures suicide prevention initiatives using a community-led, systems-based approach, targeting priority populations at risk of suicide or suicidal distress.
targetGroups: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples

name: Next Step Mental Health Coaching
description: A short-term intensive program using CBT-guided coaching to help people work through problems such as mild anxiety, depression, and day-to-day life stress.

name: Well Connect ACT Psychosocial Support Services
description: A non-clinical psychosocial support program for people aged 16–64 with severe mental illness and associated psychosocial dysfunction who are not eligible for or supported through the NDIS.

name: Alongside (Commonwealth Psychosocial Support Services)
description: Provides psychosocial support for people with severe mental illness who have comorbid issues (including substance use and/or physical health issues) and who do not have access to the NDIS or other capacity building supports.

name: Mental Health Carer Navigator
description: An online resource designed to help mental health carers navigate the ACT mental health system, providing information to support carers to understand pathways and options.

name: Mental Health First Aid (MHFA) Training
description: Two-day, face-to-face Mental Health First Aid training delivered by a certified local trainer, designed to build skills to identify and respond to signs of mental health issues and provide initial support.

name: Mental Health Direct
description: Booked mental health support sessions for people aged 16+, available for Canberra with options for telehealth and in-person sessions. Fees apply, with subsidised fees available with a GP referral.

name: Sharing Our Lived Experience (talks/workplace visits)
description: Presentations sharing the family's lived experience of suicide loss, delivered as talks or workplace visits to raise awareness and support conversations about mental health and suicide.

name: ASIST -Applied Suicide Intervention Skills Training
description: A two-day suicide intervention skills training course (subsidised), providing participants with structured training and a workbook to build confidence to recognise and respond to suicide risk.

name: GP Mental Health / Eating Disorders Treatment Plan
description: A 40-minute appointment including assessment and preparation of a GP mental health treatment plan, agreed goals, psychoeducation, crisis/relapse planning where appropriate, and arranging referrals and follow-up.

name: Next Step -Low Intensity Therapy (coaching)
description: Short-term CBT-based low intensity support (up to six coaching sessions) for mild anxiety, depression, and day-to-day life stress. Self-referral is available without a mental health care plan.

name: Next Step -Cool Little Kids (3–6) Anxiety Group
description: A 6-session CBT-based group program (via Teams) for parents of shy or anxious children aged 3–6, teaching practical skills to understand and manage child anxiety.

name: Next Step -Cool Kids (7–12) Anxiety Group
description: A 9-session CBT-based group program during school terms for children aged 7–12 and their carers, providing strategies to manage anxiety and worry.

name: Better Access -Bulk Billing Counselling Service
description: Free bulk-billing counselling (psychologists and mental health accredited social workers) for the Canberra community, assisting with depression, anxiety/stress, grief/loss, alcohol and other drug use, and sleep problems.

name: Menslink Unplugged (school education program)
description: A 1-hour education program helping young guys examine how social media and technology use can affect values, decision making and mental health, prompting reflection on time, content and impact.

name: Menslink in Organisations (presentations/talks)
description: Menslink visits organisations in the ACT and surrounding regions to present talks on young men's issues, men's mental health, mental fitness, suicide prevention, volunteering, and Menslink's work.

name: Mental Health Education Programs (workshops and courses)
description: Structured education programs and workshops that build understanding of mental health conditions, recovery, communication, and support strategies for community members, carers, and workplaces.

name: Workplace and Organisational Training
description: Training for workplaces and organisations to build mental health literacy, reduce stigma, and improve how staff recognise and respond to mental health issues in colleagues and clients.

name: Clinical Care Coordinator (service planning and navigation)
description: A coordination role that helps people identify wellbeing needs and goals, decide which services to access, and supports service planning and navigation, including help to obtain a Mental Health Care Plan if needed.

name: Psychosocial Recovery Coaching
description: Recovery coaching to support participants to build capacity, set goals, and work toward recovery, including help to connect with supports and strengthen routines over time.

name: Perinatal Counselling (pregnancy and postnatal)
description: Appointment-based counselling supporting emotional wellbeing during pregnancy and the postnatal period, including support for anxiety, depression, stress, trauma, grief and adjustment to parenthood.

name: Community and Professional Education (training)
description: Training and education sessions for health and community professionals and/or community members to build knowledge and confidence in recognising and responding to perinatal mental health issues.

name: Psychosocial Recovery Coaching
description: Recovery coaching for NDIS participants focused on building capacity, routines and confidence, supporting recovery goals and linking to supports where needed.

name: NDIS Support Coordination
description: NDIS support coordination to help participants organise and connect with supports under "Assist-Life Stage, Transition" supports.

name: NDIS Recovery Coaching
description: NDIS recovery coaching provided under "Assist-Life Stage, Transition" supports to help people pursue recovery goals with structured coaching support.

name: NDIS Peer Mentoring
description: Peer mentoring support provided under "Assist-Life Stage, Transition" supports, offering mentoring and support through lived-experience-informed connection.

name: Women Talk Shops
description: Free networking "talk shop" events for Culturally and Linguistically Diverse women providing information about social, professional and upskilling opportunities in Canberra, along with guest speakers and activities. The program aims to build connection and break stigma around mental health within Culturally and Linguistically Diverse communities.

name: Breaking the Barrier (intergenerational conversations)
description: An ACT Government funded activity involving intergenerational conversations, surveys, and one-to-one interviews with migrants and first-generation migrants, focused on mental health, intergenerational gaps, and strategies to support parents and young people.

name: Creative Writing Competitions (mental health awareness)
description: Creative writing competitions designed to create mental health awareness within Culturally and Linguistically Diverse communities.

name: Cooking Workshops (mental health awareness)
description: Cooking workshops designed to create mental health awareness within Culturally and Linguistically Diverse communities, using shared activity and learning.

name: "Have you just arrived in Canberra?" (migrant support database)
description: An online resource directing new arrivals to a migrant support database covering accommodation, culturally sensitive mental health services, legal aid, employment and skill-building workshops, support for children/families, and community connections.

name: Step Up Step Down Garran (SCSUSD)
description: A short-term residential mental health care program combining clinical support from Canberra Health Service and psychosocial support from Stride and Woden Community Service. Typical stay up to two weeks, followed by four weeks of non-residential community outreach.

name: General Practice (private practice)
description: General practice appointments providing holistic healthcare, including chronic disease management, women's health, mental health care plans and psychotherapy as required.

name: Canberra and Tuggeranong Medicare Mental Health Centres
description: Community mental health centres providing accessible mental health services via a multidisciplinary team including psychologists, nurses, occupational therapists and social workers (with psychiatry input as required).

name: Medicare Mental Health Phone Service
description: A Commonwealth-funded phone-based mental health service providing accessible support by phone for people in the Canberra region.

name: Mental Health & Wellbeing Counselling (up to 12 weeks)
description: One-on-one counselling (~50 minutes) with a qualified counsellor for up to 12 weeks, focused on safety, trust, emotional wellbeing and coping skills, with no diagnosis required.

name: Mental Health & Wellbeing Group Programs (COS-P, Healing Trauma, DBT)
description: Structured group programs (6–8 weeks) including Circle of Security Parenting, Healing Trauma Group, and Dialectical Behaviour Therapy group to build coping skills, support parenting confidence, and strengthen relationships after trauma and distress.

name: Wesley LifeForce Suicide Prevention Networks
description: Community-led suicide prevention networks that bring local people and organisations together to improve awareness, reduce stigma, and support community responses to suicide risk.

name: Commonwealth Psychosocial Support Program -New Path
description: Early intervention recovery support for people at the less complex end of severe mental illness, working towards independence and mainstream services.

name: Commonwealth Psychosocial Support Program -Continuity of Support
description: Medium to longer-term, lighter-touch psychosocial support with peer/social groups plus targeted individual assistance to reduce isolation and increase social connection.

name: Commonwealth Psychosocial Support Program -NDIS Access Support
description: Wrap-around assistance with NDIS applications for participants coming through New Path or CoS whose needs are assessed as better met through NDIS funding.

name: The Way Back Support Service
description: Support for people following a suicide attempt to help them take action to stay safe, providing psychosocial follow-up and safety-focused support in the community.

name: Health in My Language -Bilingual Health Education
description: Free bilingual health education sessions delivered by educators who speak participants' languages, designed for women from multicultural backgrounds, covering topics including mental health and navigating the mental health system.

name: Mental Health Justice Clinic
description: A free legal advice and assistance service for people with lived experience of mental ill-health, and for associated carers, friends or family members. Assists with discrimination, employment, health services, abuse, Centrelink, public housing, complaints, and appeals.

name: Alongside
description: A community-based psychosocial support program for people aged over 16 experiencing mental illness and complex issues. Provides confidential, non-judgemental support with daily living, maintaining a home, budgeting, social connection, vocational goals, wellbeing, and life skills.

name: Take Action! Mental Health Complaints and Feedback in the ACT
description: A mental health-specific guide made up of six fact sheets to help people in the ACT understand complaints and feedback options in mental health care, including how to raise concerns, make complaints, and use advocacy pathways.

name: Mental Health and Court Support
description: A non-clinical rights and case support offering for prisoners and involuntary patients that investigates complaints and follows focus cases in mental health and justice contexts.

name: Connecting with People Training Program
description: A compassion-based and evidence-informed suicide prevention training program for ACT community services workers and associated primary health care workers, including modules on compassion at work, community suicide awareness, and co-producing safety plans.

name: ACT Alcohol, Tobacco and Other Drugs – Mental Health Alliance
description: A cross-sector alliance established by MHCC ACT and ATODA to address fragmentation and gaps between alcohol and other drug services and mental health services in the ACT, working through working groups, a community of practice, and professional development.

name: Perinatal Mental Health Alliance
description: A sector alliance established by MHCC ACT to improve integration, consistency, and future planning for perinatal mental health services in the ACT, connecting member organisations and service providers.

name: Mental Health Week ACT
description: An annual ACT community mental health promotion and connection initiative bringing together sector partners, community organisations, lived experience advocates, and the broader community through activities, awards, and public events.

name: Multicultural Counselling Service
description: A free, culturally responsive counselling service for multicultural community members, including people from Culturally and Linguistically Diverse backgrounds, providing confidential talk therapy with trained counsellors.

name: Peer Support and Group Programs
description: Peer support and facilitated group programs that help people improve mental health by sharing stories, learning coping strategies, and connecting with others who understand their experiences.

name: Mental Health Matters (training course)
description: A three-hour introductory mental health training course made available through Canberra Region Presbytery to help local congregations support people with mental health problems and build knowledge.

name: MATES (befriending program)
description: A befriending program for people with a diagnosed mental illness who are ready to reconnect to their community. Volunteers support participants to work toward their goals through local social activities and community outings.

name: Community Recovery Coaching
description: A demo-only psychosocial recovery coaching program for the live CarePath provider portal sample organisation.

name: Peer Connection Groups
description: A demo-only peer group program used to show provider-side program filtering without representing a real member organisation.
